document.title: IEC 60601-1 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1 Edition 3.2 is the consolidated 2020 international general safety and essential performance standard for medical electrical equipment and systems.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used as the base IEC 60601 series standard for assessing basic safety and essential performance of ME equipment and ME systems, together with applicable collateral and particular standards.
keyPoints: Edition 3.2 consolidates the 2005 third edition, Amendment 1:2012, Amendment 2:2020, corrigenda, and interpretation sheets 1-3.
The standard applies to basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment and medical electrical systems.
Collateral standards become normative when applicable, and particular standards take priority over general and collateral requirements.
Compliance uses risk management concepts, including requirements tied to ISO 14971 and the risk management file.
The table of contents identifies major requirement areas including marking, electrical hazards, mechanical hazards, radiation, temperatures, hazardous situations, PEMS, ME systems, and EMC.

entities.name: International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC)
entities.evidence: Foreword identifies IEC as the worldwide standardization organization that publishes International Standards and IEC Publications.

entities.name: IEC Subcommittee 62A
entities.evidence: The standard was prepared by subcommittee 62A: Common aspects of electrical equipment used in medical practice.

entities.name: IEC Technical Committee 62
entities.evidence: SC 62A is part of IEC technical committee 62: Electrical equipment in medical practice.

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Cover title states IEC 60601-1, Edition 3.2 2020-08, Medical electrical equipment - Part 1.

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: Introduction states compliance with IEC 60601-1 requires the manufacturer to have a risk management process complying with parts of ISO 14971.

entities.name: IEC 62304
entities.evidence: Normative references list IEC 62304:2006, Medical device software - Software life cycle processes, Amendment 1:2015.

concepts.name: Basic safety and essential performance
concepts.evidence: Scope applies to BASIC SAFETY and ESSENTIAL PERFORMANCE of ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS.

concepts.name: Medical electrical equipment and medical electrical systems
concepts.evidence: The scope defines the subject as MEDICAL ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT and MEDICAL ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS, referred to as ME EQUIPMENT and ME SYSTEMS.

concepts.name: Collateral standards
concepts.evidence: Collateral standards specify general requirements for basic safety and essential performance for subgroups or characteristics of ME equipment.

concepts.name: Particular standards priority
concepts.evidence: Particular standards may modify, replace, delete, or add requirements, and a particular standard requirement takes priority.

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: The introduction states a general requirement was introduced to carry out a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS.

concepts.name: PEMS development life-cycle
concepts.evidence: Table of contents includes Clause 14 on Programmable Electrical Medical Systems, including development life-cycle, verification, validation, and modification.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility
concepts.evidence: Table of contents includes Clause 17, Electromagnetic compatibility of ME equipment and ME systems.

concepts.name: Mandatory requirement wording
concepts.evidence: Foreword states 'shall' means compliance with a requirement or test is mandatory for compliance with the standard.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 Edition 3.2 was published as a consolidated version in August 2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Cover identifies IEC 60601-1, Edition 3.2 2020-08, CONSOLIDATED VERSION.

evidence.claim: The document is the general standard for basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Title states Medical electrical equipment - Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance.

evidence.claim: Edition 3.2 consolidates the 2005 third edition, Amendment 1:2012, and Amendment 2:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword says edition 3.2 contains the third edition (2005-12), amendment 1 (2012-07), and amendment 2 (2020-08).

evidence.claim: The consolidated version includes corrigenda and interpretation sheets 1, 2, and 3.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword states the consolidated version includes corrigenda 1 and 2, the corrigendum to Amendment 1, and interpretation sheets 1-3.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 applies to basic safety and essential performance of ME equipment and ME systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Scope states the standard applies to BASIC SAFETY and ESSENTIAL PERFORMANCE of MEDICAL ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT and MEDICAL ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS.

evidence.claim: The standard excludes certain IVD equipment, active implantable device implantable parts, and medical gas pipeline systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Scope states the IEC 60601 series does not apply to IVD equipment outside ME equipment, implantable parts covered by ISO 14708, or medical gas pipeline systems covered by ISO 7396-1.

evidence.claim: The object of IEC 60601-1 is to specify general requirements and serve as the basis for particular standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 1.2 states the object is to specify general requirements and serve as the basis for particular standards.

evidence.claim: Applicable IEC 60601 collateral standards are normative together with IEC 60601-1.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 1.3 states applicable collateral standards become normative at publication and apply together with this standard.

evidence.claim: Declarations of conformity to IEC 60601-1 should list applied collateral standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 1.3 note says when declaring compliance, the declarer should specifically list collateral standards applied.

evidence.claim: Particular standards take priority over IEC 60601-1 and applicable collateral standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 1.4 states a requirement of a particular standard takes priority over this standard and applicable collateral standards.

evidence.claim: The third edition introduced a risk management process requirement.
evidence.evidenceText: Foreword notes the edition was restructured and added a requirement for including a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS.

evidence.claim: Compliance with IEC 60601-1 requires a manufacturer risk management process complying with parts of ISO 14971.
evidence.evidenceText: Introduction states compliance requires the MANUFACTURER to have a RISK MANAGEMENT PROCESS complying with parts of ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Amendment 2 addressed high-priority issues selected by IEC/SC 62A after the 2015 Kobe and 2016 Frankfurt meetings.
evidence.evidenceText: Introduction to Amendment 2 describes a shortlist of high-priority issues selected by National Committees for Amendment 2.

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1 includes requirements for marking, accompanying documents, electrical hazards, mechanical hazards, radiation, excessive temperatures, hazardous situations, PEMS, ME systems, and EMC.
evidence.evidenceText: Table of contents lists Clauses 7 through 17 covering these requirement areas.

evidence.claim: Normative references include EMC, usability, alarms, medical software, biocompatibility, symbols, and risk management standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Normative references list IEC 60601-1-2, IEC 60601-1-6, IEC 60601-1-8, IEC 62304, ISO 10993, ISO 15223-1, and ISO 14971.

evidence.claim: Interpretation Sheet 1 clarifies IEC 60601-1 does not apply to medical gas pipeline systems covered by ISO 7396-1.
evidence.evidenceText: Interpretation Sheet 1 under Subclause 1.1 states IEC 60601-1 does not apply to medical gas pipeline systems covered by ISO 7396-1.

evidence.claim: Interpretation Sheet 2 clarifies fire enclosure and fault testing decisions rely on manufacturer risk analysis and risk management file documentation.
evidence.evidenceText: Interpretation Sheet 2 states manufacturers should determine combustible materials through analyses documented in the RISK MANAGEMENT FILE and that risk analysis determines components subject to failure testing.

evidence.claim: Interpretation Sheet 3 provides an alternate risk-control approach for accessible enclosure surface temperatures in single fault condition.
evidence.evidenceText: Interpretation Sheet 3 permits fulfillment for enclosure surface temperatures if conditions include a 105 °C operator-accessible surface limit, IFU warning, ISO 7010-W018 where needed, and acceptable risk assessment.

reviewFlags.reason: This extraction is limited to chunk 1 of 13 and mainly covers front matter, interpretation sheets, contents, foreword, introduction, scope, and normative references.
No product-specific model names are supported by this chunk; productModels is therefore empty.
The source metadata authoritySignal says FDA, but the chunk content supports IEC as the issuing standards authority; FDA was not added as document authority.
